page: Google Cloud Platform a2-highgpu-8g is an accelerator-optimized server configured with eight NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPUs providing a total of 320 GB of VRAM. The host system features 96 vCPUs powered by dedicated Intel Xeon processors running at 2.2 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, with 48 physical cores and hyper-threading enabled. System memory totals 680.0 GB, yielding approximately 7.08 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data reveals a distinct performance profile characterized by top-tier large language model inference speeds and strong multi-core capabilities, contrasted with weaker single-core performance. The server achieves top-tier results in LLM prompt processing across 135M, 7B, and 70B models, as well as top-tier text generation speeds for 7B and 70B models. Multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth are strong, as evidenced by high stress-ng multi-core scores and uncached read bandwidth of 219.95 GB/sec. However, single-core benchmarks, including stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core tests, fall into the bottom 25%, indicating that workloads reliant on single-threaded execution will face performance bottlenecks.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the high density of GPU accelerators and dedicated compute resources positions this server for specialized, resource-intensive deployments where parallel processing justifies the premium hardware class. It is highly suited for large-scale deep learning, LLM inference, ray tracing, and high-throughput database operations, such as Redis SET operations which benchmarked in the top 25%. Conversely, it is inefficient for general-purpose web serving or single-threaded application hosting due to the underutilization of its massive GPU array and the relatively weak single-core CPU performance.
description: Google Cloud Platform a2-highgpu-8g is an accelerator-optimized server designed for highly parallelized workloads. It features eight NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPUs with 320 GB of total VRAM, paired with 96 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs (48 physical cores) and 680.0 GB of RAM. While single-core CPU performance is relatively weak, the server delivers top-tier multi-core execution, high memory bandwidth, and exceptional large language model inference speeds. This hardware profile lacks local storage and complimentary public IPv4 addresses. It is highly optimized for deep learning, LLM inference, ray tracing, and high-throughput database operations, offering high resource density for parallel computing tasks.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform a2-highgpu-8g is an accelerator-optimized server featuring 8 NVIDIA A100 GPUs, 320 GB VRAM, 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, and 680 GB RAM, built for high-performance parallel computing.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform a2-highgpu-8g is an accelerator-optimized server with 8 NVIDIA A100 GPUs, 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, and 680 GB RAM.
tagline: An accelerator-optimized platform featuring eight NVIDIA A100 GPUs and ninety-six dedicated vCPUs for demanding parallel computing and machine learning workloads.
bullet_points: Equipped with 8 NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPUs and 320 GB of total VRAM
Features 96 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 680 GB of system memory
Delivers top-tier performance for large language model inference and prompt processing
Exhibits strong multi-core CPU performance and high memory bandwidth
Tradeoff includes weak single-core CPU performance in single-threaded tasks
Best suited for deep learning, parallel computing, and high-throughput database operations
